content: Hey, I just got back from an amazing sabbatical in Eastern Europe and I'm trying to get back into a routine.
Welcome back! It can be tough to adjust after a long trip. What were some of your favorite experiences from your travels?
I loved visiting all the different cities and trying all the local foods. I'm a big fan of Thai cuisine, so I was excited to try all the different variations of dishes like pad thai and green curry.
Thai food is delicious. Did you pick up any cooking tips or recipes while you were traveling?
Yeah, I took a cooking class in Budapest and learned how to make some amazing dishes. I've been trying to recreate them at home, but it's not the same as having all the fresh ingredients available.
That sounds like a great experience. What do you like to do in your free time, now that you're back home?
I love woodworking and making things with my hands. I've been working on a few projects, like building a chess table and making some calligraphy pens.
That's really cool. I've never met anyone who's into calligraphy before. Do you have a favorite type of wood to work with?
I love working with walnut and oak. They're so versatile and have a great texture. I've also been brewing my own beer at home, which is a fun hobby.
That's a great hobby. What kind of beers do you like to brew?
I'm a big fan of IPAs and stouts. I've been experimenting with different recipes and ingredients to create some unique flavors.
That sounds like a lot of fun. Do you have a favorite beer that you've brewed so far?
Yeah, I brewed a great IPA last summer that turned out amazing. I'm thinking of entering it into a competition on August 20th.
That's exciting. Good luck with the competition. What inspired you to start brewing your own beer?
I've always been interested in trying new things and experimenting with different flavors. I started brewing beer about 5 years ago and have been hooked ever since.
It's great that you've found a hobby that you're so passionate about. Do you have any plans for upcoming trips or adventures?
Yeah, I'm thinking of planning a trip to Japan next year to visit some of the different breweries and try some of the local beers.
That sounds like an amazing trip. I'm sure you'll have a great time trying all the different beers and experiencing the culture.
Definitely. I'm really looking forward to it. I've already started making a list of all the places I want to visit and beers I want to try.
